evidence: Enterprise Data: Cloud-based and on-premises CPU servers and workstations, and artificial intelligence (AI) systems — 25.2% of total revenue in 2025
DC to DC solutions are used to convert and control voltages within... AI systems, memory, storage solutions, notebooks, infotainment, power sources
Our success depends on our investment of significant resources in research and development
Enterprise Data end market includes AI systems, contributing 25.2% of total revenue in 2025, down from 32.5% in 2024
DC to DC products used in AI systems differentiated by high degree of integration, high voltage operation, high switching speed, high energy efficiency
Regulatory restrictions target products capable of enabling or facilitating AI, and could impact sales of our products supporting AI applications
Unauthorized use of AI tools poses risks: IP and data protection, exposure of proprietary information, misuse of IP, inability to claim ownership of AI tool outputs
EU AI Act, U.S. federal/state AI laws, Executive Orders create inconsistent compliance obligations; failure to comply could subject us to legal liability and damage reputation

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No quantified AI-attributable R&D or capex figures found in excerpts; AI revenue contribution is disclosed but reflects customer demand, not MPWR's own AI investment spend. Score reflects named AI product lines without quantified investment scale.

ai_narrative_concreteness: Excerpts describe AI as an end-market application for power management products but provide no deployment metrics or quantified business outcomes from AI-specific initiatives.
capex_mentions: No dollar amounts tied to AI-attributable investment appear in the provided excerpts.
